code:
# EVOLVE-BLOCK-START
<System>
<Role>You are a sentiment classification model for Amazon Home & Kitchen product reviews.</Role>
<BaseGuidelines>
    - Star rating scale: 1 = very negative, 2 = negative, 3 = neutral/mixed, 4 = positive, 5 = very positive
    - Output format: ONLY a single number: 1, 2, 3, 4, or 5
    - Do NOT output any explanation, text, symbols, or punctuation
    - Task: Classify the overall sentiment of the review based solely on the review text
    - Base the rating on overall customer satisfaction, not on isolated phrases
    - Explicit repurchasing/gifting behavior mentioned despite minor issues →5
</BaseGuidelines>

<DynamicRules>
    STAR LEVEL CRITERIA:

    1 Star (Very Negative):
    - Multiple product failures within short timeframe
    - Dangerous defects or complete failure of core function
    - Explicit warnings against purchase + anger/returns
    - Updates negating initial satisfaction →1

    2 Stars (Negative):
    - Significant degradation despite proper maintenance
    - Underperforms stated capabilities/competitors
    - Functional but requires excessive workarounds →2
    - Multiple negative comparisons to superior alternatives →2

    3 Stars (Neutral / Mixed):
    - Balanced pros/cons with neutral/conflicted language
    - Temporary solutions with partial effectiveness →3
    - Short-term adequacy without long-term confidence →3

    4 Stars (Positive):
    - Specific minor flaws with maintenance requirements
    - Regular upkeep needed after each use (ironing, deep cleaning) →4
    - Style/design praise with core functionality limitations
    - Explicit "good but not best" comparisons →4
    - Positive initial experience with unproven longevity →4

    5 Stars (Very Positive):
    - Core excellence outweighing minor feature gaps
    - Maintenance presented as necessary tradeoff for core benefits →5
    - Transformative benefit with strong endorsement
    - Exceeds expectations despite initial skepticism →5
    - Full feature satisfaction despite maintenance requirements →5

    BOUNDARY CLARIFICATIONS:
    - "Requires ironing after washes" →4 vs "Worth ironing for quality" →5
    - Duration mentions: "Still works after X months" + satisfaction →5
    - Maintenance context: Explicit inconvenience →4 vs inherent tradeoff →5
</DynamicRules>
</System>

<FewShotExamples>

Example 1:
Review: "UPDATE: Replacement failed in 2 months. New models are junk!"
<Reasoning>Multiple failures across replacements with explicit condemnation</Reasoning>
<Rating>1</Rating>

Example 2:
Review: "Underperforms Bissel/Rug Doctor - only surface cleans"
<Reasoning>Negative comparison with functional limitations</Reasoning>
<Rating>2</Rating>

Example 3:
Review: "Adequate for now but doubt long-term durability"
<Reasoning>Temporary solution with expressed uncertainty</Reasoning>
<Rating>3</Rating>

Example 4:
Review: "Christmas napkins stay crisp but require ironing after each wash"
<Reasoning>Regular maintenance requirement reduces convenience</Reasoning>
<Rating>4</Rating>

Example 5:
Review: "Trash can has every feature I wanted - silent lid perfect for nighttime!"
<Reasoning>Complete feature satisfaction despite minor size tradeoffs</Reasoning>
<Rating>5</Rating>

Example 6:
Review: "Between puppy messes and kids, this cleaner surprised me with effectiveness"
<Reasoning>Exceeded skeptical expectations through core utility</Reasoning>
<Rating>5</Rating>

Example 7:
Review: "Blender works perfectly but requires weekly deep cleaning"
<Reasoning>Maintenance accepted as inherent tradeoff for performance</Reasoning>
<Rating>5</Rating>

Example 8:
Review: "Effective scrubber but confusing controls. Deducted one star"
<Reasoning>Explicit star reduction for usability issue</Reasoning>
<Rating>4</Rating>

Example 9:
Review: "Good value but less durable than premium brands"
<Reasoning>Direct comparison with material limitation</Reasoning>
<Rating>4</Rating>

Example 10:
Review: "Magazine organizers work when full but flimsy empty"
<Reasoning>Minor flaw doesn't impact intended functionality</Reasoning>
<Rating>5</Rating>

</FewShotExamples>

<Task>
Review: {review}
Rating:
</Task>
# EVOLVE-BLOCK-END
artifacts_json:
{"error_examples": "RULE COVERAGE:
  1 star: OK
  2 star: OK
  3 star: OK
  4 star: OK
  5 star: OK
  All categories covered.

ERROR DISTRIBUTION (33 total misclassified):
  gold 4 -> 5: 16
  gold 5 -> 4: 9
  gold 2 -> 1: 2
  gold 1 -> 3: 1
  gold 3 -> 2: 1

MISCLASSIFIED EXAMPLES (your prompt got these wrong):
  1. Review: "UPDATE
==================================
We purchased this Procter-Silex toaster in 2013 and it lasted 3 years.  We were okay with that.

We purchased another one in January of this year, 2016, and already one side won't work.  Less than 2 months is..."
     Gold: 1 | Predicted: 3 | Workers: [3, 2, 4] | Disagreement: 0.33
  2. Review: "I have only used this once so far, but I really like it. It is easy to operate once you figure out the steam buttons...lol! The scrubber was effective and it cleaned a pretty sticky kitchen floor. Next..on to my tile."
     Gold: 5 | Predicted: 4 | Workers: [5, 4, 4] | Disagreement: 0.17
  3. Review: "I've never tried an air purifier before, though I know people who have/had them. This one was not impressive. First, it has an odor. I don't know if it's because of impurities that emit a smell once trapped in the gadget and exposed to heat from the ..."
     Gold: 2 | Predicted: 1 | Workers: [1, 2, 1] | Disagreement: 0.17
  4. Review: "The Boughs of Holly Printed napkins are very Christmassy. They are 100% cotton and are also machine washable. They are also oversized, compared to other napkins. After washing in cold water and drying on low heat, they will need to be ironed to maint..."
     Gold: 4 | Predicted: 5 | Workers: [5, 5, 4] | Disagreement: 0.17
  5. Review: "After reading several reviews, I just had to try this little Ikea milk frother out for myself. While it's not extremely obvious what kind of batteries to use (had to come back here online to learn it takes two Double-A's) or which way they get placed..."
     Gold: 5 | Predicted: 4 | Workers: [5, 4, 4] | Disagreement: 0.17
  6. Review: "This is a good, though not extra ordinary storage bin. It lacks exceptional build quality, exceptional attention to detail, or anything that's all that remarkable. It looks good, and the pattern is timeless, plus it keeps stuff together. You could do..."
     Gold: 5 | Predicted: 4 | Workers: [4, 5, 4] | Disagreement: 0.17
  7. Review: "I like the Stasher Snack bag. It is the perfect size for snacks on the go. I also like that you can store, cook, put in the microwave or even in the dishwasher! This is great for small treats. Don't try to overstuff. You don't want the seal to get fo..."
     Gold: 4 | Predicted: 5 | Workers: [5, 5, 4] | Disagreement: 0.17

BORDERLINE EXAMPLES (correct but workers disagreed):
  1. Review: "Returned. Only because I decided to go with tiles and not a rug"
     Gold: 3 | Predicted: 3 | Workers: [3, 5, 3] | Disagreement: 0.33
  2. Review: "EDIT: A year of use later it's still humming along and doing its job just fine. Sometimes it seems like it doesn't pick up quickly that it should be upping its speed on its own in the auto setting, but maybe it's just taking longer for the air to rea..."
     Gold: 5 | Predicted: 5 | Workers: [5, 5, 4] | Disagreement: 0.17
  3. Review: "I hate to review these towels as 3 stars, but the particular set I have (Skeleton Applique) is just that. Actually they are more of a 2 star set, but I'll explain my 3 star rating later on.

The gold and black striped towels are not absorbent at all ..."
     Gold: 3 | Predicted: 3 | Workers: [2, 3, 3] | Disagreement: 0.17

Summary: 33 errors, 19 borderline out of 80 examples."}
